flights.note: No origin specified - flights not included in this itinerary

hotels.note: No dates specified - hotel recommendations provided but not booked
hotels.recommended_hotel: Best Western Plus Island Palms Hotel & Marina

activities.name: Cabrillo National Monument Tide Pools
activities.description: Explore incredible tide pools at the southern tip of Point Loma. Best visited during low tide (check tide charts). Kids will love discovering sea stars, anemones, crabs, and small fish in natural pools. The monument also offers stunning views and a historic lighthouse.
activities.notes: FREE - No entrance fee for pedestrians/cyclists. Vehicle entry is $20 per car (covers all passengers). Best at low tide - check NOAA tide charts before visiting.
activities.address: 1800 Cabrillo Memorial Dr, San Diego, CA 92106

activities.name: La Jolla Cove Tide Pools & Sea Lions
activities.description: Visit the famous La Jolla Cove to see wild sea lions and seals up close, then explore tide pools at nearby Shell Beach and the Children's Pool. Walk along Coast Boulevard Trail for amazing ocean views and wildlife spotting.
activities.notes: FREE - Public beach access. Parking can be challenging; metered street parking available ($2-3/hour). Best tide pooling at Shell Beach during low tide.
activities.age_appropriateness: Excellent for kids - sea lions are a huge hit

activities.name: Whale Watching Tour - Flagship Cruises
activities.description: 3-4 hour whale watching adventure on San Diego Bay. December-April see gray whales; June-September see blue whales and dolphins. Naturalist guides provide education. Indoor and outdoor viewing areas available.
activities.notes: Children under 4 are free. Tours depart from 990 N Harbor Dr. Book in advance for best rates. Bring layers as it gets cool on the water. Whale sightings guaranteed or receive a voucher for another trip.
activities.age_appropriateness: Great for ages 6 and 9 - educational and exciting
activities.address: 990 N Harbor Dr, San Diego, CA 92101

activities.name: San Diego Zoo Safari Park (Budget Alternative)
activities.description: While the full admission is $70+, consider the 'Early Morning' or 'Sunset Safari' special programs, or visit the main San Diego Zoo instead which sometimes has discount days. Both feature incredible animal encounters.
activities.notes: NOT INCLUDED due to budget constraints - Standard admission exceeds $50/person. However, kids under 3 are free, and San Diego residents get discounts. Check website for special offers.

activities.name: Birch Aquarium at Scripps
activities.description: Smaller, more intimate aquarium perfect for kids. Features local marine life, seahorses, jellyfish, and a stunning tide pool plaza with hands-on experiences. Educational programs about ocean conservation.
activities.notes: Adults $26, Children (3-17) $19.50. Total for family: $71.50. Located in La Jolla with ocean views. Can combine with La Jolla Cove visit.
activities.age_appropriateness: Perfect size for 6 and 9 year olds - not overwhelming

activities.name: Sunset Cliffs Natural Park
activities.description: FREE stunning coastal park with dramatic cliffs, tide pools (at low tide), and incredible sunset views. Great for exploring, picnicking, and tide pooling. Less crowded than La Jolla.
activities.notes: FREE - Street parking available. Be cautious near cliff edges with children. Best tide pools at the north end near Ladera Street during low tide.
activities.address: Sunset Cliffs Blvd, San Diego, CA 92107

activities.name: Living Coast Discovery Center
activities.description: Small zoo and aquarium focused on animals of San Diego Bay. Features stingrays you can touch, sea turtles, sharks, birds of prey, and a butterfly habitat. Very affordable and kid-focused.
activities.notes: Adults $15, Children (3-12) $10. Total for family: $40. Located in Chula Vista near the bay. Interactive and educational.
activities.address: 1000 Gunpowder Point Dr, Chula Vista, CA 91910

unmet_constraints: Total activity costs ($258) exceed the stated budget of $150 for 3 travelers
No origin city provided for flight booking
input.reasoning:
The user requested kid-friendly outdoor activities in San Diego with a focus on tide pools, whale watching, and animals, with a budget constraint of under $50 per person. I've created a comprehensive activity itinerary that includes:

1. FREE tide pool locations (Cabrillo National Monument, La Jolla Cove, Sunset Cliffs) - these are must-visits and cost nothing
2. Whale watching tour at $45/person ($135 total) - just under the $50/person limit
3. Additional affordable animal attractions (Birch Aquarium at $26/person, Living Coast Discovery Center at $15/person)

The challenge is that the total budget is $150 for 3 people, which is $50 per person total for the entire trip. The whale watching alone costs $135, leaving only $15 for other activities. However, with the free tide pool locations, the family can have an amazing experience focusing on:
- Whale watching tour: $135
- Free tide pools at 2-3 locations: $0
- Parking fees: ~$20-30 estimated

This would keep them close to budget while hitting all their main interests. The other paid activities (Birch Aquarium, Living Coast) are excellent options if they have flexibility in their budget.

No flights were included since no origin was specified, and no hotel bookings were made since no dates were provided.
input.final_message_to_user:
I've created a fantastic kid-friendly outdoor itinerary for San Diego focused on your interests in tide pools, whale watching, and animals!

**BUDGET CONSIDERATION:** Your total budget is $150 for 3 people ($50/person). The whale watching tour alone is $135, which uses most of your budget. However, I've included several FREE tide pool locations that will be amazing for your 6 and 9 year old!

**RECOMMENDED PLAN WITHIN BUDGET:**
✅ **Whale Watching Tour** - $135 (Flagship Cruises, $45/person)
✅ **Cabrillo National Monument Tide Pools** - FREE (or $20 per vehicle)
✅ **La Jolla Cove** - FREE (see sea lions + tide pools!)
✅ **Sunset Cliffs Natural Park** - FREE (more tide pools + stunning views)

**Total: ~$135-155** (including parking)

**IF YOU HAVE BUDGET FLEXIBILITY, ADD:**
- Living Coast Discovery Center - $40 for your family (touch stingrays!)
- Birch Aquarium - $71.50 for your family (amazing for kids)

**IMPORTANT TIPS:**
🌊 Check NOAA tide charts before visiting - tide pools are best at LOW tide
🐋 Whale season: Gray whales (Dec-Apr), Blue whales (Jun-Sep)
👟 Wear sturdy shoes with good grip for tide pooling
🧥 Bring layers for the whale watching boat - it gets chilly!

Since you didn't specify travel dates or origin city, I couldn't book flights/hotels, but I'm happy to help with that once you provide those details!
